analysis.consistency: High. The data fields are well-defined and follow standard spell documentation format.
analysis.quality: High. The spell description is detailed, covering base mechanics, reaction timing, and 'At Higher Levels' scaling.

mechanics.casting_time: 1 reaction, which you take when you see a creature within 60 feet of you casting a spell
mechanics.effect_vs_level_4_or_higher: Ability check (DC 10 + spell level) using spellcasting ability; success negates the spell.

scaling.mechanic: The spell slot level directly dictates the maximum spell level that can be automatically countered.
scaling.note: If a 4th-level slot is used, spells of 4th level or lower automatically fail. This bypasses the contested check for spells of that level.

analysis.archivist_notes: Mechanics are clear. The distinction between automatic failure (3rd level or lower) and contested check (4th level or higher) is critical for its function. Scaling ensures it remains potent against higher-level threats by increasing the automatic success threshold.
